# claudeplatform_federation_rule (Resource)

A Workload Identity Federation rule (fdrl_...): binds an issuer to a service account so matching JWTs can mint short-lived Anthropic tokens. Requires an org:admin OAuth token. OAuth callers can only manage rules scoped workspace:developer or workspace:inference; org:admin and workspace:manage_tunnels rules are Console-only by design. Destroy archives the rule.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "claudeplatform_federation_issuer" "github_actions" {
  name       = "github-actions"
  issuer_url = "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com"

  jwks {
    type = "discovery"
  }
}

resource "claudeplatform_workspace" "ml_team" {
  name = "ml-team"
}

resource "claudeplatform_service_account" "ci" {
  name = "github-actions-ci"
}

# Mint workspace:inference tokens for CI runs on the protected main branch.
resource "claudeplatform_federation_rule" "ci_inference" {
  name               = "github-actions-main"
  issuer_id          = claudeplatform_federation_issuer.github_actions.id
  service_account_id = claudeplatform_service_account.ci.id
  workspace_id       = claudeplatform_workspace.ml_team.id
  oauth_scope        = "workspace:inference"

  match {
    subject_prefix = "repo:acme/inference-service:ref:refs/heads/main"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `issuer_id` (String) Federation issuer ID (fdis_...).
- `name` (String) Name, lowercase alphanumeric plus hyphens, unique per organization.
- `service_account_id` (String) Target service account ID (svac_...). Must be a member of the rule's workspace.

### Optional

- `applies_to_all_workspaces` (Boolean) Enable the rule in every workspace in the organization.
- `match` (Block, Optional) Conditions an incoming JWT must satisfy (AND semantics). At least one of subject_prefix, claims, or condition must be set; audience alone is rejected. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--match))
- `oauth_scope` (String) Scope granted to minted tokens. API-manageable values: workspace:developer, workspace:inference. (org:admin / workspace:manage_tunnels rules must be created in the Console.)
- `token_lifetime_seconds` (Number) Lifetime of minted tokens, 60-86400 seconds. Server default 3600.
- `workspace_id` (String) Workspace the rule is enabled in at creation. Exactly one of workspace_id or applies_to_all_workspaces is required.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `match`

Optional:

- `audience` (String) The JWT aud claim must contain this exact string.
- `claims` (Map of String) Top-level claim name to required exact string value. For nested/typed claims use condition.
- `condition` (String) CEL expression over the decoded claim set (variable: claims). This is a security boundary — prefer static matchers when they express the constraint.
- `subject_prefix` (String) Exact match against the JWT sub claim; a trailing * makes it a prefix match. Case-sensitive. Pin CI rules to a protected ref — a broad wildcard like repo:org/repo:* also matches fork-triggered pull_request runs.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the federation rule ID (fdrl_...).
terraform import claudeplatform_federation_rule.ci_inference fdrl_01GhIJklm987
```
